exercises.title: AGENT BADGE
exercises.brief: Stamp an agent's name and clearance level.
promptLines: Define `interface Agent { name: string; level: number }` and **label(a: Agent): string** returning `<name> L<level>` built with a **template literal**.
starterLines: interface Agent { name: string; level: number }
// TODO: build `${name} L${level}` with a template literal
solutionLines: interface Agent { name: string; level: number }
exercises.hint: return `${a.name} L${a.level}`; — the L sits between the two interpolations.
exercises.lore: Every operator on the grid wears a badge. Yours just printed.

exercises.title: RANK SWEEP
exercises.brief: Name the highest-level agent in the array.
promptLines: With `interface Agent { name: string; level: number }`, define **topAgent(agents: Agent[]): string** returning the **name** of the agent with the highest **level**. An **empty** array returns **''**.
starterLines: interface Agent { name: string; level: number }
// TODO: guard the empty array, then find the highest level
solutionLines: interface Agent { name: string; level: number }

tests.name: keeps the first on a tie
empty -> empty string
tests.code: assertEqual(topAgent([]), '', 'no agents on the board means no name');

exercises.hint: Return '' first if the array is empty, then loop keeping the agent whose level is strictly greater.
exercises.lore: Scan the bounty board. The biggest head sets the price.

exercises.title: TUPLE FLIP
exercises.brief: Flip a fixed-shape pair end for end.
promptLines: Define **swap(p: [string, number]): [number, string]** that returns a new tuple with the two slots **flipped**: the number first, the string second.
starterLines: // TODO: return the slots in the opposite order
exercises.hint: return [p[1], p[0]]; — slot 1 (the number) goes first, slot 0 (the string) second.
exercises.lore: Reverse the packet's header and payload. The router never noticed.

exercises.title: CORE SUFFIX
exercises.brief: Append a callsign suffix, defaulting to the core.
promptLines: Define **tag(name: string, suffix?: string): string** returning `name` with `suffix` appended. When `suffix` is **omitted**, default it to **'-CORE'** using the **`??`** operator.
starterLines: function tag(name: string, suffix?: string): string {
// TODO: fall back to '-CORE' when suffix is missing
solutionLines: function tag(name: string, suffix?: string): string {

tests.name: empty string suffix is kept
tests.code: assertEqual(tag('NODE', ''), 'NODE', 'an empty string is present, so ?? does not replace it');

exercises.hint: return name + (suffix ?? '-CORE'); — ?? only falls back on null/undefined, so '' stays.
exercises.lore: Unsigned packets get stamped -CORE and routed to the deep stack.

exercises.title: GRID DISTANCE
exercises.brief: Measure a point's taxi distance from the origin.
promptLines: Define `type Point = { x: number; y: number }` and **manhattan(p: Point): number** returning the **Manhattan distance** from the origin: `Math.abs(p.x) + Math.abs(p.y)`.
starterLines: type Point = { x: number; y: number };
// TODO: sum the absolute values of x and y
solutionLines: type Point = { x: number; y: number };
exercises.hint: return Math.abs(p.x) + Math.abs(p.y); — distance is never negative.
exercises.lore: On the grid you move in right angles only. Count the blocks, not the line.
